title: mentha sylvestris var. glabra
intro: Mentha sylvestris var. glabra, also known as glabrous wild mint, is a variety of the wild mint plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It is characterized by its glabrous (hairless) leaves and stems, which distinguish it from other varieties of wild mint. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive-aiding abilities, which make it effective in treating a range of health issues, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Glabrous wild mint can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for those seeking natural health benefits.
